ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR DEC-21-2009 IN DEPARTMENT 212 AT 1:30 PM FOR FAILURE TO OBTAIN AN ANSWER(S) FROM, OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST, DEFENDANT(S) A & G LUXURY HOMES, LLC. THE NOV-13-2009 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST 'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND TO DEEM ADMITTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S FORM INTERROGATORIES WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $265, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH W. ROSE OF THE ROSE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (916)983-7840, MOVING PARTY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - ATTY. JOSEPH W. ROSE'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT SUMMIT III, LLC IS GRANTED. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And That Req.'S For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Douglas Holloway, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Bank of the West
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Bank Of The West 's Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And To Deem Admitted Plaintiff's Request For Admissions is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's Request For Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $265, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
